---
title: "`r params$modelName` — PFM Adoption Model Report"
subtitle: "Input Data, Estimation, and Future Projections for Bulk & Diffuse Adoption"
author: "Political Feasibility Module"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
params:
  cacheDir: "C:/Users/renatoro/Desktop/Input Data/remind_inputdata/cache/default"
  gdxPath: "c:/Users/renatoro/Desktop/Projects/Elevate/code/fulldata.gdx"
  assetDir: "output/adoption_model"
  modelName: "Baseline + Rule of Law"
  instQualityDrivers: !r c("Government Effectiveness (WGI)", "Rule of Law (VDem)")
  controlDrivers: !r c("GDP per Capita", "Hydro Nuclear Share")
  regionMappingFE: "regionmappingH12.csv"
  includeLagged: false
  adoptionThreshold: 0.5
  snapshotYears: !r c(2030, 2040, 2050, 2070, 2100)
  reportName: "default"
  timelineClassification: "five_groups"
  actorPowerDrivers: ~
  actorPowerIndex: "Actor Power Index"
  # Per-sector overrides (Diffuse falls back to Bulk when NULL; Bulk falls back to single params above)
  instQualityDriversBulk: ~
  controlDriversBulk: ~
  regionMappingFEBulk: ~
  includeLaggedBulk: ~
  actorPowerDriversBulk: ~
  actorPowerIndexBulk: ~
  instQualityDriversDiffuse: ~
  controlDriversDiffuse: ~
  regionMappingFEDiffuse: ~
  includeLaggedDiffuse: ~
  actorPowerDriversDiffuse: ~
  actorPowerIndexDiffuse: ~
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 1
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: hide
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 6
---

<style>
  body { background-color: #f5f7fa; color: #333; }
  .main-container { max-width: 95% !important; }
  .toc-content { padding-left: 0px !important; }
  h1, h2, h3 { color: #2C3E50; font-weight: 700; }
  .nav-tabs {
    font-weight: bold;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
    border-bottom: 2px solid #2C3E50;
  }
  .nav-tabs > li.active > a,
  .nav-tabs > li.active > a:focus,
  .nav-tabs > li.active > a:hover {
    background-color: #2C3E50; color: #fff;
    border: 1px solid #2C3E50; border-bottom-color: transparent;
  }
  .tab-content {
    padding: 25px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-top: none;
    border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; background-color: #fff;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
  }
  .card {
    background-color: #fff; border-radius: 8px;
    border: 1px solid #eef2f5; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.02);
    margin-bottom: 25px;
  }
  .card-header {
    background-color: #f8fafc; border-bottom: 1px solid #eef2f5;
    padding: 12px 20px; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.1em; color: #2C3E50;
  }
  .card-body { padding: 20px; }
  /* Sector selector bar */
  #sectorBar {
    position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 9999;
    background: #2C3E50; color: white;
    padding: 8px 24px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
    margin-bottom: 10px;
  }
  .sector-btn {
    padding: 5px 22px; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 700;
    cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.9em; transition: all 0.15s;
  }
  .sector-btn.active  { background: white !important; color: #2C3E50 !important; border: none !important; }
  .sector-btn.inactive { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15) !important; color: white !important;
                         border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.5) !important; }
  .chart-description {
    background: #f8fafc; border-left: 4px solid #2C3E50;
    padding: 10px 16px; margin: 10px 0 18px 0;
    font-size: 0.93em; color: #444; line-height: 1.55;
  }
</style>

<div id="sectorBar">
  <span style="font-weight:700;font-size:0.95em;margin-right:4px;">Sector:</span>
  <button id="btn-bulk" class="sector-btn active" onclick="setSector('Bulk')">Bulk</button>
  <button id="btn-diffuse" class="sector-btn inactive" onclick="setSector('Diffuse')">Diffuse</button>
  <span style="font-size:0.8em;opacity:0.65;margin-left:8px;">Click to show only Bulk or Diffuse results throughout the report</span>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  window.setSector = function(sector) {
    document.getElementById('btn-bulk').className    = 'sector-btn ' + (sector === 'Bulk'    ? 'active' : 'inactive');
    document.getElementById('btn-diffuse').className = 'sector-btn ' + (sector === 'Diffuse' ? 'active' : 'inactive');
    document.querySelectorAll('.bulk-only').forEach(function(el) {
      el.style.display = sector === 'Bulk' ? '' : 'none';
    });
    document.querySelectorAll('.diffuse-only').forEach(function(el) {
      el.style.display = sector === 'Diffuse' ? '' : 'none';
    });
  };
  window.addEventListener('load', function() { setSector('Bulk'); });
})();
</script>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center", dev = "svg"
)

library(pfm)
library(magclass)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)

source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/configHelper.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/theme.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/modelHelpers.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/forestPlot.R"))

if (!is.null(params$cacheDir) && nchar(params$cacheDir) > 0) {
  madrat::setConfig(
    forcecache    = TRUE,
    cachefolder   = params$cacheDir,
    mappingfolder = gsub("cache/default", "mappings", params$cacheDir)
  )
}

# Load fits from the shared PFM model store (populated by model-selection).
# Same formula + same data → same cache key → same Ridge λ → matching forest plots.
.model_store <- file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(),
                           getPfmConfig("modelDir", "models"))
dir.create(.model_store, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
options(pfm.modelDir = .model_store)

plotOutputDir <- file.path(params$assetDir, "plots")
if (!dir.exists(plotOutputDir)) dir.create(plotOutputDir, recursive = TRUE)

resolve_sector_adp <- function(sector_val, bulk_val) {
  if (is.null(sector_val) || (is.character(sector_val) && length(sector_val) == 0))
    bulk_val else sector_val
}
resolve_fe_adp <- function(val) {
  if (is.null(val) || nchar(as.character(val)) == 0) NULL else val
}
resolve_ai_adp <- function(val, default = "Actor Power Index") {
  if (is.null(val) || !nzchar(as.character(val))) default else val
}

# Bulk defaults: sector-specific param → old single param
bulk_iq  <- resolve_sector_adp(params$instQualityDriversBulk, params$instQualityDrivers)
bulk_ctl <- resolve_sector_adp(params$controlDriversBulk,     params$controlDrivers)
bulk_fe  <- resolve_fe_adp(resolve_sector_adp(params$regionMappingFEBulk,  params$regionMappingFE))
bulk_lag <- isTRUE(resolve_sector_adp(params$includeLaggedBulk,  params$includeLagged))
bulk_ap  <- resolve_sector_adp(params$actorPowerDriversBulk,  params$actorPowerDrivers)
bulk_ai  <- resolve_ai_adp(resolve_sector_adp(params$actorPowerIndexBulk, params$actorPowerIndex))

cfg <- list(
  Bulk = list(
    instQuality = bulk_iq,
    controls    = bulk_ctl,
    regionFE    = bulk_fe,
    lagged      = bulk_lag,
    actorPower  = bulk_ap,
    actorIndex  = bulk_ai
  ),
  Diffuse = list(
    instQuality = resolve_sector_adp(params$instQualityDriversDiffuse, bulk_iq),
    controls    = resolve_sector_adp(params$controlDriversDiffuse,     bulk_ctl),
    regionFE    = resolve_fe_adp(resolve_sector_adp(params$regionMappingFEDiffuse,  params$regionMappingFEBulk  %||% params$regionMappingFE)),
    lagged      = isTRUE(resolve_sector_adp(params$includeLaggedDiffuse,  bulk_lag)),
    actorPower  = resolve_sector_adp(params$actorPowerDriversDiffuse,  bulk_ap),
    actorIndex  = resolve_ai_adp(resolve_sector_adp(params$actorPowerIndexDiffuse, bulk_ai))
  )
)

# Legacy single-model vars (Bulk values; used by non-sector-specific sections)
model_instQualityDrivers <- bulk_iq
model_controlDrivers     <- bulk_ctl
model_regionMappingFE    <- bulk_fe
model_includeLagged      <- bulk_lag
model_actorPowerDrivers  <- bulk_ap
model_actorPowerIndex    <- bulk_ai
adoption_threshold       <- as.numeric(params$adoptionThreshold)
snapshot_years           <- as.integer(params$snapshotYears)

# Variables shown in the input data section — union across both sectors
model_vars_input <- c(
  "Actor Power Index|Bulk",
  "Actor Power Index|Diffuse",
  unique(c(cfg$Bulk$instQuality, cfg$Diffuse$instQuality)),
  unique(c(cfg$Bulk$controls,    cfg$Diffuse$controls))
)

# Resolve gdxPath as an absolute path relative to the project root if it is relative
gdx_path <- params$gdxPath
if (!is.null(gdx_path) && nchar(gdx_path) > 0 && !is_absolute_path(gdx_path)) {
  gdx_path <- file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), gdx_path)
}
has_scenario <- !is.null(gdx_path) && nchar(gdx_path) > 0 && file.exists(gdx_path)

tidy_magpie <- function(mag_obj, period_label = "Historical") {
  if (is.null(mag_obj)) return(NULL)
  df      <- as.data.frame(mag_obj)
  reg_col <- if ("Region" %in% colnames(df)) "Region" else colnames(df)[1]
  yr_col  <- if ("Year"   %in% colnames(df)) "Year"   else colnames(df)[2]
  var_col <- if ("Data1"  %in% colnames(df)) "Data1"  else colnames(df)[3]
  val_col <- if ("Value"  %in% colnames(df)) "Value"  else colnames(df)[4]
  data.frame(
    region   = trimws(as.character(df[[reg_col]])),
    year     = as.integer(gsub("y", "", as.character(df[[yr_col]]))),
    variable = trimws(as.character(df[[var_col]])),
    value    = as.numeric(df[[val_col]]),
    period   = period_label,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

key_regions <- c("USA", "CHA", "DEU", "IND", "JPN", "BRA", "RUS", "ZAF")
```

```{r load_data, cache=FALSE, include=FALSE}
panelData <- getPanelDataHistoricalCached(
  aggregate = TRUE, y = 2000:2022,
  outputRegionMappingFile = "regionmapping_54.csv"
)

df_hist <- tidy_magpie(panelData, "Historical")

future_mag <- NULL
df_scen    <- NULL
if (has_scenario) {
  future_mag <- getPanelDataScenarioCached(
    gdxFile = gdx_path,
    aggregate = TRUE,
    outputRegionMappingFile = "regionmapping_54.csv"
  )
  df_scen <- tidy_magpie(future_mag, "Scenario")
}

df_all <- do.call(rbind, Filter(Negate(is.null), list(df_hist, df_scen)))
```

```{r fit_models, cache=FALSE, include=FALSE}
fit_bulk <- estimateAdoptionModel(
  data                      = panelData,
  sector                    = "Bulk",
  actorPowerDrivers         = cfg$Bulk$actorPower,
  actorPowerIndex           = cfg$Bulk$actorIndex,
  instQualityDrivers        = cfg$Bulk$instQuality,
  controlDrivers            = cfg$Bulk$controls,
  includeLaggedAdoption     = cfg$Bulk$lagged,
  interactRegionFE          = FALSE,
  regionMappingFixedEffects = cfg$Bulk$regionFE
)

fit_diffuse <- estimateAdoptionModel(
  data                      = panelData,
  sector                    = "Diffuse",
  actorPowerDrivers         = cfg$Diffuse$actorPower,
  actorPowerIndex           = cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex,
  instQualityDrivers        = cfg$Diffuse$instQuality,
  controlDrivers            = cfg$Diffuse$controls,
  includeLaggedAdoption     = cfg$Diffuse$lagged,
  interactRegionFE          = FALSE,
  regionMappingFixedEffects = cfg$Diffuse$regionFE
)

future_df_bulk    <- NULL
future_df_diffuse <- NULL

if (!is.null(future_mag)) {
  future_df_bulk <- preparePanelData(
    data                      = future_mag,
    sector                    = "Bulk",
    actorPowerDrivers         = cfg$Bulk$actorPower,
    actorPowerIndex           = cfg$Bulk$actorIndex,
    instQualityDrivers        = cfg$Bulk$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = cfg$Bulk$controls,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = cfg$Bulk$regionFE,
    lag                       = 1
  )
  future_df_diffuse <- preparePanelData(
    data                      = future_mag,
    sector                    = "Diffuse",
    actorPowerDrivers         = cfg$Diffuse$actorPower,
    actorPowerIndex           = cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex,
    instQualityDrivers        = cfg$Diffuse$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = cfg$Diffuse$controls,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = cfg$Diffuse$regionFE,
    lag                       = 1
  )
}

compute_fit_stats <- function(fit) {
  m          <- fit$model
  is_logistf <- inherits(m, "logistf")
  k          <- length(coef(m))
  nObs       <- if (is_logistf) m$n else nobs(m)
  loglik     <- if (is_logistf) as.numeric(m$loglik["full"]) else as.numeric(logLik(m))
  aic        <- if (is_logistf) -2 * loglik + 2 * k else AIC(m)
  bic        <- if (is_logistf) -2 * loglik + k * log(nObs) else BIC(m)
  pseudoR2   <- if (is_logistf) {
    round(1 - (m$loglik["full"] / m$loglik["null"]), 3)
  } else if (!is.null(m$deviance) && !is.null(m$null.deviance)) {
    round(1 - (m$deviance / m$null.deviance), 3)
  } else NA
  pvals <- if (!is.null(fit$coeftest)) fit$coeftest[, 4] else NULL
  nSig  <- if (!is.null(pvals)) sum(pvals < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE) else NA
  list(
    k = k, nObs = nObs,
    loglik   = round(loglik, 2),
    aic      = round(aic, 2),
    bic      = round(bic, 2),
    pseudoR2 = pseudoR2,
    nSig     = nSig,
    converged = if (is_logistf) !any(is.na(m$coefficients)) else isTRUE(m$converged)
  )
}

stats_bulk    <- compute_fit_stats(fit_bulk)
stats_diffuse <- compute_fit_stats(fit_diffuse)

get_probs <- function(fit, future_df) {
  if (is.null(fit) || is.null(future_df)) return(NULL)
  m           <- fit$model
  needed_vars <- intersect(all.vars(m$formula), colnames(future_df))
  df_clean    <- future_df[complete.cases(future_df[, needed_vars, drop = FALSE]), ]
  if (nrow(df_clean) == 0) return(NULL)
  pred_link   <- tryCatch(predict(m, newdata = df_clean), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(pred_link)) return(NULL)
  df_clean$prob <- 1 / (1 + exp(-pred_link))
  df_clean
}

proj_bulk    <- get_probs(fit_bulk,    future_df_bulk)
proj_diffuse <- get_probs(fit_diffuse, future_df_diffuse)
```

---

# Executive Summary

This report documents a single-model deep dive into the **`r params$modelName`** specification of the PFM adoption hurdle model. It covers both the **Bulk** (industrial) and **Diffuse** (transport/buildings) sectors and includes three sections: input data inspection (historical & scenario), model estimation diagnostics, and future adoption projections.

<div class="row">
<div class="col-md-4 bulk-only">
<div class="card">
<div class="card-header" style="border-left:4px solid #2980b9;">Bulk Sector Model</div>
<div class="card-body">
<ul>
  <li><strong>Model name:</strong> `r params$modelName`</li>
  <li><strong>Response:</strong> Binary adoption (ECP_Bulk > 0) — Firth penalised logit</li>
  <li><strong>Actor Power:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Bulk$actorIndex, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>IQ drivers:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Bulk$instQuality, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Controls:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Bulk$controls, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Region FE:</strong> `r if (is.null(cfg$Bulk$regionFE)) "None" else cfg$Bulk$regionFE`</li>
  <li><strong>Lagged adoption:</strong> `r cfg$Bulk$lagged`</li>
</ul>
</div></div></div>
<div class="col-md-4 diffuse-only">
<div class="card">
<div class="card-header" style="border-left:4px solid #27ae60;">Diffuse Sector Model</div>
<div class="card-body">
<ul>
  <li><strong>Model name:</strong> `r params$modelName`</li>
  <li><strong>Response:</strong> Binary adoption (ECP_Diffuse > 0) — Firth penalised logit</li>
  <li><strong>Actor Power:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>IQ drivers:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Diffuse$instQuality, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Controls:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Diffuse$controls, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Region FE:</strong> `r if (is.null(cfg$Diffuse$regionFE)) "None" else cfg$Diffuse$regionFE`</li>
  <li><strong>Lagged adoption:</strong> `r cfg$Diffuse$lagged`</li>
</ul>
</div></div></div>
<div class="col-md-4">
<div class="card">
<div class="card-header">Projection Settings</div>
<div class="card-body">
<ul>
  <li><strong>Adoption threshold:</strong> `r adoption_threshold` (Pr(Adoption) &ge; threshold &rarr; adopted)</li>
  <li><strong>Snapshot years:</strong> `r paste(snapshot_years, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Scenario data:</strong> `r if (has_scenario) paste0("Loaded from ", basename(params$gdxPath)) else "Not provided — projection sections disabled"`</li>
  <li><strong>Historical range:</strong> 2000–2022</li>
</ul>
</div></div></div>
</div>

```{r summary-fit-table, echo=FALSE}
summary_df <- data.frame(
  Sector     = c("Bulk", "Diffuse"),
  `Pseudo-R²`= c(stats_bulk$pseudoR2, stats_diffuse$pseudoR2),
  AIC        = c(stats_bulk$aic,      stats_diffuse$aic),
  BIC        = c(stats_bulk$bic,      stats_diffuse$bic),
  `Log-lik`  = c(stats_bulk$loglik,   stats_diffuse$loglik),
  `N obs`    = c(stats_bulk$nObs,     stats_diffuse$nObs),
  `N pred`   = c(stats_bulk$k,        stats_diffuse$k),
  `N sig`    = c(stats_bulk$nSig,     stats_diffuse$nSig),
  Converged  = c(stats_bulk$converged, stats_diffuse$converged),
  check.names = FALSE
)

kable(summary_df, format = "html", caption = "Adoption Model Fit Summary") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(2, bold = TRUE,
    color = ifelse(summary_df$`Pseudo-R²` >= 0.4, col_good,
      ifelse(summary_df$`Pseudo-R²` >= 0.2, col_warn, col_bad)))
```

---

# Data Quality Check

```{r data-qa, echo=FALSE, results='asis', cache=FALSE}
check_panel_nas <- function(mag_obj, label) {
  if (is.null(mag_obj)) {
    cat(sprintf(
      '<div style="background:#fef9e7;border-left:4px solid %s;padding:10px 16px;margin-bottom:12px;">',
      col_warn
    ))
    cat(sprintf('<strong style="color:%s;">⊘ %s</strong>: not loaded.</div>\n', col_warn, label))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # Use as.array() — magclass's as.data.frame() uses sparse representation and
  # silently drops NaN/NA cells, giving false-clean results. The full array
  # includes every region × year × variable cell, including NaN.
  arr      <- as.array(mag_obj)
  n_total  <- length(arr)
  bad_mask <- !is.finite(arr)
  n_bad    <- sum(bad_mask)

  if (n_bad == 0) {
    cat(sprintf(
      '<div style="background:#eafaf1;border-left:4px solid %s;padding:10px 16px;margin-bottom:12px;">',
      col_good
    ))
    cat(sprintf(
      '<strong style="color:%s;">&#10003; %s</strong>: no missing or non-finite values across %s variable–region–year combinations.</div>\n',
      col_good, label, format(n_total, big.mark = ",")
    ))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  pct     <- round(100 * n_bad / n_total, 2)
  cat(sprintf(
    '<div style="background:#fef9e7;border-left:4px solid %s;padding:10px 16px;margin-bottom:4px;">',
    col_warn
  ))
  cat(sprintf(
    '<strong style="color:%s;">&#9888; %s</strong>: %s non-finite values (NA / NaN / Inf) — %.2f%% of %s total.</div>\n',
    col_warn, label, format(n_bad, big.mark = ","), pct, format(n_total, big.mark = ",")
  ))

  dn       <- dimnames(arr)
  bad_idx  <- which(bad_mask, arr.ind = TRUE)
  bad_vals <- arr[bad_mask]
  bad_df   <- data.frame(
    region   = dn[[1]][bad_idx[, 1]],
    year     = as.integer(gsub("y", "", dn[[2]][bad_idx[, 2]])),
    variable = dn[[3]][bad_idx[, 3]],
    kind     = ifelse(is.nan(bad_vals), "NaN",
                 ifelse(is.na(bad_vals), "NA",
                   ifelse(bad_vals == Inf, "Inf", "-Inf"))),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  by_var <- bad_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(variable, kind) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      `Count`            = dplyr::n(),
      `Affected regions` = paste(sort(unique(region)), collapse = ", "),
      `Years`            = if (min(year) == max(year)) as.character(min(year))
                           else paste0(min(year), "–", max(year)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(`Count`))

  print(
    knitr::kable(by_var, format = "html",
                 caption = paste0(label, " — non-finite values by variable")) %>%
      kableExtra::kable_styling(
        bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
        full_width = TRUE
      )
  )
  invisible(NULL)
}

check_panel_nas(panelData,  "Historical panel")
check_panel_nas(future_mag, "Scenario panel")

# --- Region coverage: which regions appear in each stage of the pipeline ---
banner <- function(ok, msg) {
  bg  <- if (ok) "#eafaf1" else "#fef9e7"
  col <- if (ok) col_good  else col_warn
  ico <- if (ok) "&#10003;" else "&#9888;"
  cat(sprintf(
    '<div style="background:%s;border-left:4px solid %s;padding:10px 16px;margin-bottom:8px;"><strong style="color:%s;">%s %s</strong></div>\n',
    bg, col, col, ico, msg
  ))
}

hist_regions <- sort(magclass::getItems(panelData, dim = 1))

if (!is.null(future_mag)) {
  scen_regions <- sort(magclass::getItems(future_mag, dim = 1))
  missing_in_scen <- setdiff(hist_regions, scen_regions)
  extra_in_scen   <- setdiff(scen_regions, hist_regions)

  cat("\n\n**Region coverage — Historical vs Scenario panel:**\n\n")
  if (length(missing_in_scen) == 0) {
    banner(TRUE, sprintf("Scenario panel covers all %d historical regions.", length(hist_regions)))
  } else {
    banner(FALSE, sprintf(
      "%d region(s) present in historical panel but ABSENT from scenario panel: %s",
      length(missing_in_scen), paste(missing_in_scen, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  if (length(extra_in_scen) > 0) {
    banner(FALSE, sprintf(
      "%d region(s) in scenario panel but NOT in historical panel: %s",
      length(extra_in_scen), paste(extra_in_scen, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
}

# Coverage and year-count after complete.cases() inside get_probs()
check_proj_coverage <- function(proj_df, hist_regs, label) {
  if (is.null(proj_df)) return(invisible(NULL))

  expected_years <- length(unique(proj_df$year))
  yr_counts <- proj_df %>%
    dplyr::count(region, name = "n_years")

  proj_regs <- sort(yr_counts$region)
  missing   <- setdiff(hist_regs, proj_regs)
  sparse    <- yr_counts %>%
    dplyr::filter(n_years < expected_years) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(n_years)

  cat(sprintf("\n\n**Region coverage after model prediction — %s:**\n\n", label))

  if (length(missing) == 0) {
    banner(TRUE, sprintf("All %d historical regions are present in %s projections.", length(hist_regs), label))
  } else {
    banner(FALSE, sprintf(
      "%d region(s) dropped before/after prediction in %s: %s",
      length(missing), label, paste(missing, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }

  if (nrow(sparse) == 0) {
    banner(TRUE, sprintf("All regions have the full %d projected years.", expected_years))
  } else {
    banner(FALSE, sprintf(
      "%d region(s) have fewer than the expected %d projected years (sparse rows may cause near-zero-only trajectories):",
      nrow(sparse), expected_years
    ))
    print(
      knitr::kable(sparse, col.names = c("Region", "Years with predictions"),
                   format = "html",
                   caption = paste0(label, " — regions with incomplete year coverage")) %>%
        kableExtra::kable_styling(
          bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
          full_width = FALSE
        )
    )
  }
}

check_proj_coverage(proj_bulk,    hist_regions, "Bulk")
check_proj_coverage(proj_diffuse, hist_regions, "Diffuse")
```

---

# Input Data {.tabset}

Variables used across both sector models: **`r paste(unique(c(cfg$Bulk$actorIndex, cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex)), collapse = "**, **")`** (Actor Power), **`r paste(unique(c(cfg$Bulk$instQuality, cfg$Diffuse$instQuality)), collapse = "**, **")`** (IQ), **`r paste(unique(c(cfg$Bulk$controls, cfg$Diffuse$controls)), collapse = "**, **")`** (Controls).

## Historical Distributions

```{r hist-distributions, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
df_model_hist <- df_hist %>%
  filter(variable %in% model_vars_input)

df_model_hist$var_short <- df_model_hist$variable %>%
  gsub("\\|Bulk",   " (Bulk)",   .) %>%
  gsub("\\|Diffuse", " (Diffuse)", .) %>%
  gsub(" \\(WGI\\)", " [WGI]", .) %>%
  gsub(" \\(VDem\\)", " [VDem]", .)

ggplot(df_model_hist, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_density(fill = "#2C7BB6", alpha = 0.6, color = "white") +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.2, color = "#2C3E50") +
  facet_wrap(~var_short, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    title    = paste0(params$modelName, " — Historical Variable Distributions (2000–2022)"),
    subtitle = "Aggregated 54-region panel; density estimated over region-year observations",
    x = "Variable value", y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_report()
```

### Summary Statistics

```{r hist-stats, echo=FALSE}
hist_stats <- df_model_hist %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  summarize(
    N      = sum(!is.na(value)),
    Mean   = mean(value,   na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD     = sd(value,     na.rm = TRUE),
    Min    = min(value,    na.rm = TRUE),
    Max    = max(value,    na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kable(hist_stats, digits = 3, caption = "Historical Variable Statistics (Aggregated, 2000–2022)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE)
```

## Historical Trends

```{r hist-trends, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
df_trends <- df_model_hist %>%
  filter(region %in% key_regions)

df_trends$var_short <- df_trends$variable %>%
  gsub("\\|Bulk",   " (Bulk)",   .) %>%
  gsub("\\|Diffuse", " (Diffuse)", .) %>%
  gsub(" \\(WGI\\)", " [WGI]", .) %>%
  gsub(" \\(VDem\\)", " [VDem]", .)

ggplot(df_trends, aes(x = year, y = value, color = region)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~var_short, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    title    = paste0(params$modelName, " — Historical Trends by Region (2000–2022)"),
    subtitle = "Selected major economies; aggregated 54-region mapping",
    x = "Year", y = "Variable value", color = "Region"
  ) +
  theme_report() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Scenario Projections

```{r scenario-projections, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
if (has_scenario) {
  df_model_all <- df_all %>%
    filter(variable %in% model_vars_input, region %in% key_regions)

  df_model_all$var_short <- df_model_all$variable %>%
    gsub("\\|Bulk",    " (Bulk)",    .) %>%
    gsub("\\|Diffuse", " (Diffuse)", .) %>%
    gsub(" \\(WGI\\)", " [WGI]", .) %>%
    gsub(" \\(VDem\\)", " [VDem]", .)

  ggplot(df_model_all, aes(x = year, y = value, color = region, linetype = period)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 2022, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    facet_wrap(~var_short, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0(params$modelName, " — Historical & Scenario Variable Trajectories"),
      subtitle = "Vertical dotted line = 2022 (historical / scenario boundary)",
      x = "Year", y = "Variable value", color = "Region", linetype = "Period"
    ) +
    theme_report() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
} else {
  cat("> [!NOTE] Scenario GDX file not provided — long-term projections are disabled.")
}
```

---

# Model Estimation

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```

```{r forest-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
coef_df_bulk <- pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_bulk$coeftest) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

p_bulk <- render_forest_plot(
  coef_df_bulk,
  title    = "Bulk Adoption — Coefficient Forest Plot",
  subtitle = sprintf(
    "Log-odds coefficients | Pseudo-R² = %.3f | AIC = %.1f | %d/%d significant",
    stats_bulk$pseudoR2, stats_bulk$aic, stats_bulk$nSig, stats_bulk$k
  )
)

if (!is.null(p_bulk)) {
  print(p_bulk)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_bulk_adoption.png"), p_bulk, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_bulk_adoption.svg"), p_bulk, width = 10, height = 6)
}
```

### Coefficient Table

```{r coef-table-bulk, echo=FALSE}
coef_display_bulk <- pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_bulk$coeftest) %>%
  mutate(
    term = vapply(term, clean_term_plain, character(1)),
    Significance = case_when(
      pValue < 0.001 ~ "***", pValue < 0.01 ~ "**",
      pValue < 0.05  ~ "*",   pValue < 0.1  ~ ".", TRUE ~ ""
    )
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Term = term, Estimate = estimate,
    `Std. Error` = stdError, `z / t` = statistic, `p-value` = pValue
  )

kable(coef_display_bulk, digits = 4, format = "html",
  caption = "Bulk Adoption — Coefficient Estimates") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(5, bold = TRUE,
    color = ifelse(!is.na(coef_display_bulk$`p-value`) & coef_display_bulk$`p-value` < 0.05, col_good, "grey50"))
```

### Fit Diagnostics

```{r diag-bulk, echo=FALSE}
diag_bulk_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Pseudo-R²", "AIC", "BIC", "Log-likelihood",
             "N observations", "N predictors", "N significant (p<0.05)", "Converged"),
  Value = c(
    stats_bulk$pseudoR2, stats_bulk$aic, stats_bulk$bic, stats_bulk$loglik,
    stats_bulk$nObs, stats_bulk$k, stats_bulk$nSig,
    as.character(stats_bulk$converged)
  )
)

kable(diag_bulk_df, format = "html", caption = "Bulk Adoption — Model Diagnostics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```

```{r empty-h2-diffuse-est, include=FALSE}
# placeholder — diffuse content follows directly
```

```{r forest-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
coef_df_diffuse <- pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_diffuse$coeftest) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

p_diffuse <- render_forest_plot(
  coef_df_diffuse,
  title    = "Diffuse Adoption — Coefficient Forest Plot",
  subtitle = sprintf(
    "Log-odds coefficients | Pseudo-R² = %.3f | AIC = %.1f | %d/%d significant",
    stats_diffuse$pseudoR2, stats_diffuse$aic, stats_diffuse$nSig, stats_diffuse$k
  )
)

if (!is.null(p_diffuse)) {
  print(p_diffuse)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_diffuse_adoption.png"), p_diffuse, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_diffuse_adoption.svg"), p_diffuse, width = 10, height = 6)
}
```

### Coefficient Table

```{r coef-table-diffuse, echo=FALSE}
coef_display_diffuse <- pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_diffuse$coeftest) %>%
  mutate(
    term = vapply(term, clean_term_plain, character(1)),
    Significance = case_when(
      pValue < 0.001 ~ "***", pValue < 0.01 ~ "**",
      pValue < 0.05  ~ "*",   pValue < 0.1  ~ ".", TRUE ~ ""
    )
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Term = term, Estimate = estimate,
    `Std. Error` = stdError, `z / t` = statistic, `p-value` = pValue
  )

kable(coef_display_diffuse, digits = 4, format = "html",
  caption = "Diffuse Adoption — Coefficient Estimates") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(5, bold = TRUE,
    color = ifelse(!is.na(coef_display_diffuse$`p-value`) & coef_display_diffuse$`p-value` < 0.05, col_good, "grey50"))
```

### Fit Diagnostics

```{r diag-diffuse, echo=FALSE}
diag_diffuse_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Pseudo-R²", "AIC", "BIC", "Log-likelihood",
             "N observations", "N predictors", "N significant (p<0.05)", "Converged"),
  Value = c(
    stats_diffuse$pseudoR2, stats_diffuse$aic, stats_diffuse$bic, stats_diffuse$loglik,
    stats_diffuse$nObs, stats_diffuse$k, stats_diffuse$nSig,
    as.character(stats_diffuse$converged)
  )
)

kable(diag_diffuse_df, format = "html", caption = "Diffuse Adoption — Model Diagnostics") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

---

# Historical Model Fit {.tabset}

```{r hist-fit-helper, include=FALSE}
build_hist_fit <- function(fit, sector, data) {
  sec_cfg <- cfg[[sector]]
  df <- pfm::preparePanelData(
    data                      = data,
    sector                    = sector,
    actorPowerDrivers         = sec_cfg$actorPower,
    actorPowerIndex           = sec_cfg$actorIndex,
    instQualityDrivers        = sec_cfg$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = sec_cfg$controls,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = sec_cfg$regionFE,
    lag                       = 1
  )
  df$adoption_raw <- as.integer(df$ecp > 0)

  needed_vars <- intersect(all.vars(fit$model$formula), colnames(df))
  ok          <- complete.cases(df[, needed_vars, drop = FALSE])
  df$fitted_prob <- NA_real_
  pred <- tryCatch(
    predict(fit$model, newdata = df[ok, ], type = "link"),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (!is.null(pred)) df$fitted_prob[ok] <- 1 / (1 + exp(-as.numeric(pred)))
  df
}

hist_fit_bulk    <- build_hist_fit(fit_bulk,    "Bulk",    panelData)
hist_fit_diffuse <- build_hist_fit(fit_diffuse, "Diffuse", panelData)

# --- Trajectory plot (adopted or non-adopted regions) ---
make_traj_plot <- function(df, sector_name, adopted_group = TRUE) {
  col_sector  <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  group_label <- if (adopted_group) "Adopted" else "Non-Adopted"

  ever_adopted <- df %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarise(adopted = any(adoption_raw == 1, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  regs <- ever_adopted %>% filter(adopted == adopted_group) %>% pull(region)
  df_f <- df %>% filter(region %in% regs)
  if (nrow(df_f) == 0) return(NULL)

  n_regs <- length(unique(df_f$region))
  ncols  <- min(6L, n_regs)

  ggplot(df_f, aes(x = year)) +
    geom_step(aes(y = adoption_raw, color = "Actual adoption (0/1)"),
              linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_line(aes(y = fitted_prob, color = "Model fitted probability"),
              linewidth = 1.0, na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~region, ncol = ncols) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Actual adoption (0/1)" = "grey55",
                 "Model fitted probability" = col_sector),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0(sector_name, " — ", group_label, " Regions: In-Sample Fit"),
      subtitle = "Grey step = actual adoption (0/1); solid = model fitted probability",
      x = "Year", y = "Probability / Adoption"
    ) +
    theme_report() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}

# --- Density plot of predicted probabilities by actual outcome ---
make_density_plot <- function(df, sector_name) {
  df_clean <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(fitted_prob)) %>%
    mutate(outcome = factor(
      ifelse(adoption_raw == 1, "Adopted", "Not Adopted"),
      levels = c("Adopted", "Not Adopted")
    ))

  ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = fitted_prob, fill = outcome, color = outcome)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Adopted" = "#E74C3C", "Not Adopted" = "#3498DB"),
                      name = "Actual Outcome") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Adopted" = "#E74C3C", "Not Adopted" = "#3498DB"),
                       name = "Actual Outcome") +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Model Fit: Adoption Probability (", sector_name, ")"),
      subtitle = "Distribution of predicted probabilities for actual outcomes",
      x        = "Predicted Probability of Adopting a Carbon Price",
      y        = "Density",
      caption  = paste0(
        "Figure: Fit of the Logit (adoption) model component.\n",
        "Shows predicted probability distributions for actual adoption outcomes.\n",
        "Colors indicate the observed outcome (adopted vs not adopted)."
      )
    ) +
    theme_report() +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8, color = "grey40"))
}

# --- Adoption timeline (Gantt chart) ---
make_timeline_plot <- function(df, sector_name, threshold) {
  col_sector <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse

  adopted_regs <- df %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    filter(any(adoption_raw == 1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    ungroup()

  actual <- adopted_regs %>%
    filter(adoption_raw == 1) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarise(start_year = min(year), end_year = max(year), .groups = "drop")

  predicted <- adopted_regs %>%
    filter(!is.na(fitted_prob), fitted_prob >= threshold) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarise(pred_year = min(year), .groups = "drop")

  timeline <- actual %>%
    left_join(predicted, by = "region") %>%
    arrange(start_year, region)

  timeline$region <- factor(timeline$region, levels = rev(timeline$region))

  ggplot(timeline, aes(y = region)) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = start_year, xend = end_year, yend = region,
                     color = "Real Adoption"),
                 linewidth = 5, alpha = 0.45, lineend = "round") +
    geom_point(aes(x = pred_year, color = "Predicted Adoption"),
               size = 3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Real Adoption" = "grey55", "Predicted Adoption" = col_sector),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Carbon Price Adoption: Real vs Predicted (",
                        sector_name, ", threshold = ", threshold, ")"),
      subtitle = "Grey bar = actual adoption period; dot = first year model predicts adoption",
      x = "Year", y = NULL,
      caption  = paste0(
        "Note: Predicted adoption based on probability > ", threshold, ".\n",
        "Logit model only predicts adoption probability, not price levels."
      )
    ) +
    theme_report() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8, color = "grey40"))
}
```

## Adopted Regions

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r hist-traj-bulk-adopted, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
p <- make_traj_plot(hist_fit_bulk, "Bulk", adopted_group = TRUE)
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_fit_bulk_adopted.png"), p, width=14, height=10, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each panel is one region historically observed to adopt a carbon price. The grey step function is the actual 0/1 adoption indicator; the solid coloured line is the model's fitted adoption probability. Good fit means the probability rises and stays high during the observed adoption period. Where the line is high before the step, the model anticipated adoption early; where the line stays low despite observed adoption, the model under-predicts for that region.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r hist-traj-diffuse-adopted, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
p <- make_traj_plot(hist_fit_diffuse, "Diffuse", adopted_group = TRUE)
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_fit_diffuse_adopted.png"), p, width=14, height=10, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Same interpretation as Bulk — adopted regions for the Diffuse (transport/buildings) sector. Diffuse-sector adoption is typically rarer and more politically contested, so the model's discrimination may differ from the Bulk sector.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

## Non-Adopted Regions

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r hist-traj-bulk-nonadopted, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
p <- make_traj_plot(hist_fit_bulk, "Bulk", adopted_group = FALSE)
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_fit_bulk_nonadopted.png"), p, width=14, height=10, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Regions that have never adopted a Bulk carbon price. The model's fitted probability should remain low throughout. Regions where the probability drifts high despite no observed adoption are "near misses" — the model thinks conditions are favourable even though adoption has not yet occurred. These are policy-relevant signals of regions that may be close to the adoption threshold.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r hist-traj-diffuse-nonadopted, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
p <- make_traj_plot(hist_fit_diffuse, "Diffuse", adopted_group = FALSE)
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_fit_diffuse_nonadopted.png"), p, width=14, height=10, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Non-adopted regions for the Diffuse sector. The probability should stay low. High-probability non-adopters are regions where diffuse-sector carbon pricing conditions are structurally favourable but political barriers have so far prevented adoption.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

## Probability Distribution

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r hist-density-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
p <- make_density_plot(hist_fit_bulk, "Bulk")
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_density_bulk.png"), p, width=10, height=6, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Density distributions of the model's fitted adoption probabilities, separated by whether the region actually adopted (red) or not (blue). Good model discrimination means the two distributions are well-separated: the "Adopted" distribution is skewed toward high probabilities and "Not Adopted" toward low. Overlap between the two curves indicates regions the model finds ambiguous.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r hist-density-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
p <- make_density_plot(hist_fit_diffuse, "Diffuse")
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_density_diffuse.png"), p, width=10, height=6, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Same interpretation as Bulk — separation between adopted (red) and non-adopted (blue) probability distributions indicates model discriminatory power for the Diffuse sector.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

## Adoption Timeline

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r hist-timeline-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=11, fig.height=9}
p <- make_timeline_plot(hist_fit_bulk, "Bulk", adoption_threshold)
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_timeline_bulk.png"), p, width=11, height=9, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** The grey bar spans each region's historical adoption period (first to last year with positive ECP_Bulk). The coloured dot is the first year the model's fitted probability exceeded the adoption threshold. A dot to the LEFT of the bar start means the model predicted adoption before it actually happened (early warning). A dot to the RIGHT means the model was late — the region adopted before the model's conditions were met. Regions without a dot never exceeded the threshold in sample.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r hist-timeline-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=11, fig.height=9}
p <- make_timeline_plot(hist_fit_diffuse, "Diffuse", adoption_threshold)
if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_timeline_diffuse.png"), p, width=11, height=9, dpi=300) }
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Same timeline interpretation for the Diffuse sector. The dot–bar alignment reveals whether the model correctly anticipates the timing of Diffuse sector adoption for each region.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

---

# Model Interpretation {.tabset}

## Average Marginal Effects

Average Marginal Effects (AMEs) translate log-odds coefficients into the probability scale.
The AME for predictor k is the average, across all observations, of the instantaneous slope
of Pr(Adoption) with respect to x_k:
**AME_k = mean_i[ π_i(1−π_i) ] × β_k**.
Standard errors are derived via the delta method using the same robust clustered variance-covariance
matrix as the main coefficient table. An AME of 0.05 means that a one-unit increase in the
predictor raises adoption probability by ~5 percentage points on average across the sample.

```{r ame-table, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
format_ame_table <- function(fit_obj, sector_label) {
  ame_df <- fit_obj$ame
  if (is.null(ame_df) || nrow(ame_df) == 0) {
    cat(paste0("> AME not available for ", sector_label, " sector.\n"))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  ame_df <- ame_df[ame_df$term != "(Intercept)", ]
  ame_df$signif <- ifelse(!is.na(ame_df$p) & ame_df$p < 0.001, "***",
                   ifelse(!is.na(ame_df$p) & ame_df$p < 0.01,  "**",
                   ifelse(!is.na(ame_df$p) & ame_df$p < 0.05,  "*",
                   ifelse(!is.na(ame_df$p) & ame_df$p < 0.1,   ".", ""))))
  ame_df$ci95 <- sprintf("[%.4f, %.4f]", ame_df$lower, ame_df$upper)
  out <- ame_df[, c("term_group", "term", "ame", "se", "z", "p", "signif", "ci95")]
  out$term <- vapply(out$term, clean_term_plain, character(1))
  colnames(out) <- c("Group", "Term", "AME", "SE", "z", "p-value", "", "95% CI")
  out <- out[order(out$Group, out$Term), ]

  kable(out, format = "html", digits = 4,
        caption = paste(sector_label, "Sector — Average Marginal Effects on Pr(Adoption)")) %>%
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "bordered"),
      full_width = TRUE, font_size = 12
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
    column_spec(3, color = ifelse(!is.na(out$AME) & out$AME > 0, col_good,
                              ifelse(!is.na(out$AME) & out$AME < 0, col_bad, "black")))
}

cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
format_ame_table(fit_bulk,    "Bulk")
cat('</div>')
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
format_ame_table(fit_diffuse, "Diffuse")
cat('</div>')
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this table:** The Average Marginal Effect (AME) for each predictor is the average slope of Pr(Adoption) with respect to that variable across all observations. An AME of 0.05 means that a 1-unit increase in the predictor is associated with a +5 percentage-point increase in adoption probability, on average across all observed region-years. Green = positive effect, red = negative effect. Significance uses the same robust clustered standard errors as the coefficient table.
</div>

## Predicted Probability Plots

Each panel shows how Pr(Adoption) changes as a single theory-group predictor sweeps from
its observed minimum to its maximum **holding all other predictors at their sample means**.
This answers: *"How does the model's predicted adoption probability respond to the full
range of [variable], keeping everything else average?"* The shaded ribbon is a delta-method
95% confidence interval. The dashed horizontal line marks the adoption threshold.

```{r margins-plots, echo=FALSE, fig.height=4, fig.width=12, results='asis'}
make_margins_panel <- function(fit_obj, sector_label, sector_col) {
  pp <- fit_obj$predictedProbs
  if (is.null(pp) || length(pp) == 0) {
    cat(paste0("> Predicted probability profiles not available for ", sector_label, ".\n"))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  col_grp <- c(
    "Actor Power"  = "#e74c3c",
    "Inst. Quality" = "#2ecc71",
    "Interaction"  = "#f1c40f"
  )

  plots <- lapply(names(pp), function(var) {
    df  <- pp[[var]]
    grp <- pfm::classifyTermGroups(var,
             actorPowerDrivers = model_actorPowerDrivers, actorPowerIndex = model_actorPowerIndex,
             instQualityDrivers = model_instQualityDrivers,
             controlDrivers = model_controlDrivers)
    col_line <- if (!is.na(col_grp[grp])) col_grp[[grp]] else sector_col
    lbl <- clean_term_plain(var)

    ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = prob)) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = col_line, alpha = 0.15) +
      geom_line(color = col_line, linewidth = 1.3) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = adoption_threshold, linetype = "dashed",
                 color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.7) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
      labs(title = lbl, subtitle = sector_label, x = lbl, y = "Pr(Adoption)") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(color = sector_col, size = 10))
  })

  n     <- length(plots)
  ncols <- min(n, 4)
  print(
    patchwork::wrap_plots(plots, ncol = ncols) +
      patchwork::plot_annotation(
        title    = paste0(sector_label, " Sector — Predicted Probability Profiles"),
        subtitle = "Sweep from observed min to max; ribbon = delta-method 95% CI; dashed = adoption threshold"
      )
  )
}

library(patchwork)
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
make_margins_panel(fit_bulk,    "Bulk",    col_bulk)
cat('</div>')
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
make_margins_panel(fit_diffuse, "Diffuse", col_diffuse)
cat('</div>')
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read these charts:** Each panel sweeps one theory predictor from its observed minimum to maximum while holding all others at their sample means. The solid line is the predicted Pr(Adoption); the shaded ribbon is the delta-method 95% CI. The dashed horizontal line marks the adoption threshold. A steep, monotonic curve with a narrow ribbon indicates the predictor strongly and precisely drives adoption. A flat curve indicates the predictor has little effect (or the effect is highly uncertain). Where the curve crosses the dashed threshold line shows the level of the predictor needed for the model to predict adoption on average.
</div>

## Partial Regression Plots

Each panel shows the component-plus-residual plot for a single theory-group predictor. In logistic regression, this represents the predictor's marginal contribution to the estimated log-odds of adoption ($\eta = \log(p / (1-p))$), after removing the linear contributions of all other variables. The slope of the red line is equal to the estimated model coefficient.

```{r partial-plots-adoption, echo=FALSE, fig.height=4, fig.width=12, results='asis'}
make_partial_regression_plots_adoption <- function(fit, sector_name, instQualityDrivers, actorPowerDrivers, actorPowerIndex) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m     <- fit$model
  train <- m$model
  ct    <- fit$coeftest
  if (is.null(ct) || is.null(train)) return(NULL)
  beta  <- coef(m)
  
  # For logistf/GLM, we have fitted.values and y
  y     <- m$y
  p     <- m$fitted.values
  if (is.null(y) || is.null(p)) return(NULL)
  
  # Compute working residuals: (y - p) / (p * (1 - p))
  # Capping the denominator to avoid division by zero or extreme outliers
  denom <- p * (1 - p)
  denom[denom < 0.01] <- 0.01
  resid <- (y - p) / denom
  
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse

  # Theory predictors: Actor Power Index + IQ (continuous, non-interaction, non-FE)
  theory_vars <- make.names(c(actorPowerIndex, instQualityDrivers))
  avail <- intersect(theory_vars, colnames(train))
  if (length(avail) == 0) return(NULL)

  plots <- lapply(avail, function(v) {
    if (!v %in% names(beta)) return(NULL)
    x_vals  <- train[[v]]
    y_vals  <- beta[v] * x_vals + resid    # component + residual
    df_cr   <- data.frame(x = x_vals, y = y_vals, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    
    # Fitted line: just the component (beta * x)
    x_range <- seq(min(x_vals, na.rm = TRUE), max(x_vals, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 100)
    df_line  <- data.frame(x = x_range, y = beta[v] * x_range)
    
    # Clean term name for label
    lbl <- clean_term_plain(v)
    
    # Crop the y axis to keep 95% of data to hide extreme outliers
    y_quantiles <- quantile(y_vals, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
    y_min <- min(y_quantiles[1], min(df_line$y)) - 1
    y_max <- max(y_quantiles[2], max(df_line$y)) + 1
    
    ggplot(df_cr, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_point(color = col_sec, alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
      geom_line(data = df_line, aes(x = x, y = y), color = col_bad, linewidth = 1) +
      coord_cartesian(ylim = c(y_min, y_max)) +
      labs(x = lbl, y = "Component + Residual (Log-Odds)") +
      theme_report() +
      theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 9))
  })
  
  plots <- Filter(Negate(is.null), plots)
  if (length(plots) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  wrap_plots(plots, ncol = min(3, length(plots))) +
    patchwork::plot_annotation(
      title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Partial Regression Plots", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Component + working residual on log-odds scale for theory predictors. Slope = model coefficient."
    )
}

cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
p_bulk <- make_partial_regression_plots_adoption(
  fit_bulk, "Bulk",
  cfg$Bulk$instQuality, cfg$Bulk$actorPower, cfg$Bulk$actorIndex
)
if (!is.null(p_bulk)) {
  print(p_bulk)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "partial_bulk_adoption.png"), p_bulk, width=12, height=4, dpi=300)
}
cat('</div>')

cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
p_diffuse <- make_partial_regression_plots_adoption(
  fit_diffuse, "Diffuse",
  cfg$Diffuse$instQuality, cfg$Diffuse$actorPower, cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex
)
if (!is.null(p_diffuse)) {
  print(p_diffuse)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "partial_diffuse_adoption.png"), p_diffuse, width=12, height=4, dpi=300)
}
cat('</div>')
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read these charts:** The partial regression plot displays the relationship between the log-odds of adoption and a single predictor, after adjusting for the linear effects of all other variables. The red line represents the estimated model slope ($\beta_j$); its slope equals the coefficient. The points are the observed responses adjusted for other covariates (component plus capped working residuals). A clear upward or downward alignment of points along the line shows that the predictor has a strong linear effect on the log-odds of adoption. Capping is applied to the residuals to prevent extreme misclassifications from distorting the plot scale.
</div>

---

# Future Projections {.tabset}

```{r proj-check, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!has_scenario) {
  cat("> [!WARNING]\n> **Scenario GDX file not provided.** All projection sections below are disabled.\n> Re-render with `gdxPath` pointing to a valid `fulldata.gdx` to enable projections.\n\n")
}
```

## Probability Trajectories

```{r prob-trajectory-helper, include=FALSE}
make_trajectory_plot <- function(proj_df, sector_name, key_regs, threshold, full_facet = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(proj_df) || nrow(proj_df) == 0) return(NULL)

  col_sector <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  all_regs   <- unique(proj_df$region)
  highlight  <- intersect(key_regs, all_regs)

  proj_df$grp <- ifelse(proj_df$region %in% highlight,
                        as.character(proj_df$region), "Other")
  proj_df$grp <- factor(proj_df$grp, levels = c(highlight, "Other"))

  my_colors <- setNames(
    c(scales::hue_pal()(length(highlight)), "grey80"),
    c(highlight, "Other")
  )

  if (full_facet) {
    ggplot(proj_df, aes(x = year, y = prob, group = region)) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
      geom_line(color = col_sector, linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
      facet_wrap(~region, ncol = 9) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
      labs(
        title    = sprintf("%s Adoption — All Regions Probability Trajectories", sector_name),
        subtitle = sprintf("Dashed line = threshold %.2f", threshold),
        x = "Year", y = "Pr(Adoption)"
      ) +
      theme_report() +
      theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 8), strip.text = element_text(size = 8))
  } else {
    ggplot(proj_df, aes(x = year, y = prob, group = region, color = grp,
        alpha = I(ifelse(grp == "Other", 0.35, 1)),
        linewidth = I(ifelse(grp == "Other", 0.5, 1.2)))) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
      geom_line() +
      scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
      labs(
        title    = sprintf("%s Adoption — Projected Probability (Key Regions)", sector_name),
        subtitle = sprintf("Dashed line = adoption threshold %.2f | Grey = all other regions", threshold),
        x = "Year", y = "Pr(Adoption)", color = "Region"
      ) +
      theme_report() +
      theme(legend.position = "right")
  }
}
```

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r traj-bulk-key, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_bulk)) {
  p <- make_trajectory_plot(proj_bulk, "Bulk", key_regions, adoption_threshold, full_facet = FALSE)
  if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "prob_trajectory_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300) }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each line is one region's projected Pr(Adoption) trajectory under the scenario. Key regions are highlighted in distinct colours; all others are grey. The dashed horizontal line marks the adoption threshold — regions whose probability crosses this line are projected to adopt. A steep upward trajectory means fast-approaching adoption; a flat line near zero means adoption is not projected in the scenario window.
</div>
```{r traj-bulk-full, echo=FALSE, fig.width=16, fig.height=20, class.output="fold-hide"}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_bulk)) {
  p_full <- make_trajectory_plot(proj_bulk, "Bulk", key_regions, adoption_threshold, full_facet = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(p_full)) print(p_full)
}
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r traj-diffuse-key, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_diffuse)) {
  p <- make_trajectory_plot(proj_diffuse, "Diffuse", key_regions, adoption_threshold, full_facet = FALSE)
  if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "prob_trajectory_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300) }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Same interpretation as Bulk — projected Pr(Diffuse Adoption) for all regions. Diffuse-sector adoption is typically slower and more heterogeneous across regions due to different political economy dynamics for transport and building sectors.
</div>
```{r traj-diffuse-full, echo=FALSE, fig.width=16, fig.height=20, class.output="fold-hide"}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_diffuse)) {
  p_full <- make_trajectory_plot(proj_diffuse, "Diffuse", key_regions, adoption_threshold, full_facet = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(p_full)) print(p_full)
}
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

## Adoption Timeline

```{r timeline-helper, include=FALSE}
# Rogers (2003) Diffusion of Innovations or custom 5-group timeline
# Breakpoints are recomputed from the actual distribution of adoption years
# for each scenario run — no hardcoded calendar dates.

.class_type <- if (!is.null(params$timelineClassification)) params$timelineClassification else "five_groups"

.current_levels <- c("Innovators", "Early Adopters", "Early Majority",
                    "Late Majority", "Laggards", "Non-Adopters")
.current_colors <- c(
  "Innovators"     = "#1a9641",
  "Early Adopters" = "#74c476",
  "Early Majority" = "#fed976",
  "Late Majority"  = "#fd8d3c",
  "Laggards"       = "#d73027",
  "Non-Adopters"   = "#969696"
)

compute_five_groups <- function(finite_years) {
  tbl <- table(finite_years)
  u_years <- as.numeric(names(tbl))
  u_counts <- as.numeric(tbl)
  m <- length(u_years)
  N <- sum(u_counts)
  
  if (m < 5) {
    boundaries <- u_years[-m]
    labels <- character(m)
    for (i in 1:m) {
      if (i == 1) {
        if (m == 1) {
          labels[i] <- paste0("Year ", u_years[i])
        } else {
          labels[i] <- paste0("≤ ", u_years[1])
        }
      } else if (i == m) {
        labels[i] <- paste0("> ", u_years[m-1])
      } else {
        labels[i] <- paste0(u_years[i-1] + 1, "–", u_years[i])
      }
    }
    return(list(
      boundaries = boundaries,
      labels = labels,
      levels = c("Innovators", "Early Adopters", "Early Majority", "Late Majority", "Laggards")[1:m],
      num_groups = m
    ))
  }
  
  cum_counts <- cumsum(u_counts)
  best_cost <- Inf
  best_indices <- NULL
  
  for (i1 in 1:(m-4)) {
    s1 <- cum_counts[i1]
    for (i2 in (i1+1):(m-3)) {
      s2 <- cum_counts[i2] - cum_counts[i1]
      for (i3 in (i2+1):(m-2)) {
        s3 <- cum_counts[i3] - cum_counts[i2]
        for (i4 in (i3+1):(m-1)) {
          s4 <- cum_counts[i4] - cum_counts[i3]
          s5 <- N - cum_counts[i4]
          
          cost <- (s1 - N/5)^2 + (s2 - N/5)^2 + (s3 - N/5)^2 + (s4 - N/5)^2 + (s5 - N/5)^2
          if (cost < best_cost) {
            best_cost <- cost
            best_indices <- c(i1, i2, i3, i4)
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  boundaries <- u_years[best_indices]
  b_int <- as.integer(round(boundaries))
  
  labels <- character(5)
  labels[1] <- paste0("≤ ", b_int[1])
  for (r in 2:4) {
    start_y <- b_int[r-1] + 1
    end_y <- b_int[r]
    if (start_y == end_y) {
      labels[r] <- as.character(end_y)
    } else {
      labels[r] <- paste0(start_y, "–", end_y)
    }
  }
  labels[5] <- paste0("≥ ", b_int[4] + 1)
  
  return(list(
    boundaries = boundaries,
    labels = labels,
    levels = c("Innovators", "Early Adopters", "Early Majority", "Late Majority", "Laggards"),
    num_groups = 5
  ))
}

compute_timeline <- function(proj_df, threshold) {
  if (is.null(proj_df) || nrow(proj_df) == 0) return(NULL)

  tl <- proj_df %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarise(
      AdoptionYear = ifelse(any(prob >= threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
                            min(year[prob >= threshold], na.rm = TRUE), Inf),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  finite_years <- tl$AdoptionYear[is.finite(tl$AdoptionYear)]
  fmt_yr       <- function(y) as.integer(round(y))

  # Require at least 2 adopters to compute meaningful breakpoints
  if (length(finite_years) < 2) {
    return(tl %>% mutate(
      Category  = ifelse(is.finite(AdoptionYear), .current_levels[1], "Non-Adopters"),
      YearRange = ifelse(is.finite(AdoptionYear),
                         as.character(fmt_yr(AdoptionYear)), "Never"),
      RegYear   = ifelse(is.finite(AdoptionYear),
                         paste0(region, " (", fmt_yr(AdoptionYear), ")"), region)
    ))
  }

  if (.class_type == "five_groups") {
    res_five <- compute_five_groups(finite_years)
    boundaries <- res_five$boundaries
    labels <- res_five$labels
    levels_five <- res_five$levels
    
    idx_vals <- findInterval(tl$AdoptionYear, boundaries, left.open = TRUE) + 1
    
    tl <- tl %>% mutate(
      Category  = ifelse(!is.finite(AdoptionYear), "Non-Adopters", levels_five[idx_vals]),
      YearRange = ifelse(!is.finite(AdoptionYear), "Never", labels[idx_vals]),
      RegYear   = ifelse(is.finite(AdoptionYear),
                         paste0(region, " (", fmt_yr(AdoptionYear), ")"), region)
    )
    return(tl)
  }

  if (.class_type == "rogers_quantile") {
    n_adopters    <- length(finite_years)
    p1 <- 0.025; p2 <- 0.16; p3 <- 0.50; p4 <- 0.84

    adopter_rows    <- which(is.finite(tl$AdoptionYear))
    sorted_idx      <- order(finite_years)
    sorted_years    <- finite_years[sorted_idx]
    rank_pcts       <- (seq_len(n_adopters) - 1) / n_adopters

    tl$rank_pct <- NA_real_
    tl$rank_pct[adopter_rows[sorted_idx]] <- rank_pcts

    b1 <- approx(rank_pcts, sorted_years, xout = p1, rule = 2)$y
    b2 <- approx(rank_pcts, sorted_years, xout = p2, rule = 2)$y
    b3 <- approx(rank_pcts, sorted_years, xout = p3, rule = 2)$y
    b4 <- approx(rank_pcts, sorted_years, xout = p4, rule = 2)$y

    result <- tl %>% mutate(
      Category = case_when(
        !is.finite(AdoptionYear) ~ "Non-Adopters",
        rank_pct < p1            ~ "Innovators",
        rank_pct < p2            ~ "Early Adopters",
        rank_pct < p3            ~ "Early Majority",
        rank_pct < p4            ~ "Late Majority",
        is.finite(AdoptionYear)  ~ "Laggards",
        TRUE                     ~ "Non-Adopters"
      ),
      YearRange = case_when(
        Category == "Innovators"     ~ paste0("< ",      fmt_yr(b1)),
        Category == "Early Adopters" ~ paste0(fmt_yr(b1), "–", fmt_yr(b2)),
        Category == "Early Majority" ~ paste0(fmt_yr(b2), "–", fmt_yr(b3)),
        Category == "Late Majority"  ~ paste0(fmt_yr(b3), "–", fmt_yr(b4)),
        Category == "Laggards"       ~ paste0("≥ ",  fmt_yr(b4)),
        TRUE                         ~ "Never"
      ),
      RegYear = ifelse(is.finite(AdoptionYear),
                       paste0(region, " (", fmt_yr(AdoptionYear), ")"), region)
    )
    return(result %>% select(-rank_pct))
  }

  # rogers_sigma
  mu    <- mean(finite_years)
  sigma <- sd(finite_years)
  if (sigma < 1) sigma <- 1

  b1 <- mu - 2 * sigma
  b2 <- mu -     sigma
  b3 <- mu
  b4 <- mu +     sigma

  tl %>% mutate(
    Category = case_when(
      !is.finite(AdoptionYear) ~ "Non-Adopters",
      AdoptionYear  < b1       ~ "Innovators",
      AdoptionYear  < b2       ~ "Early Adopters",
      AdoptionYear  < b3       ~ "Early Majority",
      AdoptionYear  < b4       ~ "Late Majority",
      TRUE                     ~ "Laggards"
    ),
    YearRange = case_when(
      Category == "Innovators"     ~ paste0("< ",      fmt_yr(b1)),
      Category == "Early Adopters" ~ paste0(fmt_yr(b1), "–", fmt_yr(b2)),
      Category == "Early Majority" ~ paste0(fmt_yr(b2), "–", fmt_yr(b3)),
      Category == "Late Majority"  ~ paste0(fmt_yr(b3), "–", fmt_yr(b4)),
      Category == "Laggards"       ~ paste0("≥ ",  fmt_yr(b4)),
      TRUE                         ~ "Never"
    ),
    RegYear = ifelse(is.finite(AdoptionYear),
                     paste0(region, " (", fmt_yr(AdoptionYear), ")"), region)
  )
}

render_timeline_table <- function(tl_df, sector_name) {
  if (is.null(tl_df)) {
    cat("> Scenario data not available.")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  final_table <- tl_df %>%
    mutate(Category = factor(Category, levels = .current_levels)) %>%
    group_by(Category, YearRange) %>%
    summarise(Regions = paste(RegYear, collapse = ", "), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(Category) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Category))

  row_colors <- unname(.current_colors[as.character(final_table$Category)])

  cat(as.character(
    kable(final_table, format = "html",
          col.names = c("Category", "Year Range", "Regions")) %>%
      kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                    full_width = TRUE) %>%
      column_spec(1, bold = TRUE, color = row_colors)
  ))
  cat("\n\n")
}

make_full_gantt_plot <- function(hist_df, proj_df, sector_name, threshold) {
  col_sector <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse

  hist_actual <- hist_df %>%
    filter(adoption_raw == 1) %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarise(hist_start = min(year), hist_end = max(year), .groups = "drop")

  proj_adoption <- if (!is.null(proj_df) && nrow(proj_df) > 0) {
    proj_df %>%
      group_by(region) %>%
      summarise(
        proj_year = ifelse(any(prob >= threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
                           min(year[prob >= threshold], na.rm = TRUE), NA_real_),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  } else {
    data.frame(region = character(0), proj_year = numeric(0))
  }

  all_regions <- unique(c(as.character(hist_df$region),
                           if (!is.null(proj_df)) as.character(proj_df$region)))
  gantt <- data.frame(region = all_regions, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    left_join(hist_actual,    by = "region") %>%
    left_join(proj_adoption,  by = "region") %>%
    filter(!is.na(hist_start) | !is.na(proj_year)) %>%
    mutate(
      sort_key = case_when(
        !is.na(hist_start) ~ as.numeric(hist_start),
        !is.na(proj_year)  ~ as.numeric(proj_year) + 1000,
        TRUE               ~ Inf
      )
    ) %>%
    arrange(sort_key) %>%
    mutate(region = factor(region, levels = rev(region)))

  max_hist_year <- max(hist_df$year, na.rm = TRUE)

  p <- ggplot(gantt, aes(y = region)) +
    geom_segment(
      data = gantt %>% filter(!is.na(hist_start)),
      aes(x = hist_start, xend = pmax(hist_end, max_hist_year), yend = region,
          color = "Historical Adoption"),
      linewidth = 4, alpha = 0.55, lineend = "round"
    ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = gantt %>% filter(!is.na(proj_year)),
      aes(x = proj_year, xend = 2150, yend = region,
          color = "Projected Adoption"),
      linewidth = 3, alpha = 0.35, lineend = "round"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Historical Adoption" = "grey55", "Projected Adoption" = col_sector),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0(sector_name, " Carbon Price Adoption: Historical + Projected"),
      subtitle = paste0("Grey = observed adoption period; Colour = projected onset (prob ≥ ", threshold, ")"),
      x = "Year", y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_report() +
    theme(
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 7)
    )

  class_type <- if (!is.null(params$timelineClassification)) params$timelineClassification else "five_groups"
  if (class_type == "five_groups" && !is.null(proj_df) && nrow(proj_df) > 0) {
    tl <- compute_timeline(proj_df, threshold)
    finite_years <- tl$AdoptionYear[is.finite(tl$AdoptionYear)]
    if (length(finite_years) >= 2) {
      res_five <- compute_five_groups(finite_years)
      if (res_five$num_groups == 5) {
        boundaries <- res_five$boundaries
        labels <- res_five$labels
        
        midpoints <- numeric(5)
        midpoints[1] <- (min(finite_years) + boundaries[1]) / 2
        midpoints[2] <- (boundaries[1] + boundaries[2] + 1) / 2
        midpoints[3] <- (boundaries[2] + boundaries[3] + 1) / 2
        midpoints[4] <- (boundaries[3] + boundaries[4] + 1) / 2
        midpoints[5] <- (boundaries[4] + max(finite_years) + 1) / 2
        
        p <- p + geom_vline(xintercept = boundaries + 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.6)
        
        label_df <- data.frame(
          x = midpoints,
          y = nrow(gantt) * 0.9,
          label = c("Innovators", "Early Adopters", "Early Majority", "Late Majority", "Laggards"),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
        p <- p + geom_text(data = label_df, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
                           angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey35", fontface = "bold", size = 3.2)
      }
    }
  }

  p
}
```

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r timeline-bulk, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_bulk)) {
  tl_bulk <- compute_timeline(proj_bulk, adoption_threshold)
  render_timeline_table(tl_bulk, "Bulk")
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this table:** Regions are categorised into Rogers' (2003) Diffusion of Innovations framework based on their projected adoption year relative to the distribution of all adopting regions. **Innovators** adopt first; **Laggards** adopt last; **Non-Adopters** never cross the threshold in the scenario window. The year range shows when each category is projected to adopt.
</div>
```{r timeline-gantt-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=16}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_bulk)) {
  p <- make_full_gantt_plot(hist_fit_bulk, proj_bulk, "Bulk", adoption_threshold)
  if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "gantt_full_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=16, dpi=300) }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each row is one region. Grey bars show the historical adoption period (years with observed ECP_Bulk &gt; 0). Coloured bars show the projected adoption period from scenario onset. This combined view reveals continuity between historical and projected adoption and highlights regions that are historically new adopters vs long-established pricing systems.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r timeline-diffuse, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_diffuse)) {
  tl_diffuse <- compute_timeline(proj_diffuse, adoption_threshold)
  render_timeline_table(tl_diffuse, "Diffuse")
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this table:** Rogers' diffusion categories for Diffuse sector adoption. Compare with the Bulk timeline to see whether the same regions are innovators or laggards in both sectors, or whether sector-specific political economy creates different adoption orderings.
</div>
```{r timeline-gantt-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=16}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_diffuse)) {
  p <- make_full_gantt_plot(hist_fit_diffuse, proj_diffuse, "Diffuse", adoption_threshold)
  if (!is.null(p)) { print(p); ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "gantt_full_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=16, dpi=300) }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Historical + projected Diffuse sector adoption timeline. The chart structure mirrors the Bulk Gantt but reflects the typically narrower set of Diffuse sector adopters.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

## Adoption Year Maps

```{r adoption-map-setup, include=FALSE}
mapping_54 <- NULL
world      <- NULL

if (has_scenario) {
  mapping_54 <- tryCatch(
    madrat::toolGetMapping("regionmapping_54.csv", type = "regional", where = "mappingfolder"),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (!is.null(mapping_54)) {
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(sf))
    world           <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "small", returnclass = "sf")
    world$iso_key   <- as.character(world$adm0_a3)
  }
}

render_adoption_year_map <- function(tl_df, sector_name, threshold) {
  if (is.null(tl_df) || is.null(mapping_54) || is.null(world)) {
    cat("> Map rendering requires scenario data and the regionmapping_54.csv file.\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # Use the canonical Rogers levels/colours defined in the timeline-helper chunk
  adopt_data <- tl_df %>%
    mutate(Category = factor(Category, levels = .current_levels))

  map_data_df <- mapping_54 %>%
    mutate(
      RegionCode  = as.character(RegionCode),
      CountryCode = as.character(CountryCode),
      CountryCode = ifelse(CountryCode == "SSD", "SDS", CountryCode)
    ) %>%
    left_join(adopt_data, by = c("RegionCode" = "region"))

  world_data <- world %>% left_join(map_data_df, by = c("iso_key" = "CountryCode"))

  # Only include levels that are actually present — prevents empty legend entries
  present_levels <- intersect(.current_levels,
                               as.character(unique(world_data$Category[!is.na(world_data$Category)])))

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = world_data[world_data$iso_key == "ATA", ],
            fill = "grey90", color = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
    geom_sf(data = world_data[world_data$iso_key != "ATA", ],
            aes(fill = Category), color = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values    = .current_colors[present_levels],
      limits    = present_levels,
      na.value  = "grey90", na.translate = FALSE
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text  = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("Projected Adoption Timeline — %s (%s)", sector_name, params$modelName),
      subtitle = sprintf("Threshold: Pr(Adoption) ≥ %.2f", threshold),
      fill     = "Projected Adoption Timeline"
    )

  print(p)
  fname <- sprintf("map_adoption_year_%s", tolower(sector_name))
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, paste0(fname, ".png")), p, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, paste0(fname, ".svg")), p, width = 12, height = 6)
}
```

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
```
```{r map-adoption-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_bulk)) {
  tl_bulk <- compute_timeline(proj_bulk, adoption_threshold)
  render_adoption_year_map(tl_bulk, "Bulk", adoption_threshold)
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this map:** Countries are coloured by their Rogers' adoption category — green shades are early adopters; orange/red are late adopters; grey means no adoption projected in the scenario window. The category boundaries are computed from the actual distribution of projected adoption years, so they adapt to the scenario's adoption pace.
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
```
```{r map-adoption-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_diffuse)) {
  tl_diffuse <- compute_timeline(proj_diffuse, adoption_threshold)
  render_adoption_year_map(tl_diffuse, "Diffuse", adoption_threshold)
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this map:** Adoption year map for the Diffuse sector. Compare with the Bulk map to identify regional patterns — does the same geography lead or lag in both sectors, or do different regions lead Diffuse vs Bulk adoption?
</div>
```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat('</div>')
```

## Probability Snapshot Maps {.tabset}

Maps show the predicted adoption probability per region at each snapshot year, using a continuous colour scale (red = low, green = high). The adoption threshold is overlaid as a reference.

```{r snapshot-map-helper, include=FALSE}
render_snapshot_map <- function(proj_df, snap_year, sector_name, threshold) {
  if (is.null(proj_df) || is.null(mapping_54) || is.null(world)) return(NULL)

  available_years <- sort(unique(proj_df$year))
  used_year <- snap_year
  if (!snap_year %in% available_years) {
    used_year <- available_years[which.min(abs(available_years - snap_year))]
  }

  df_year <- proj_df %>% filter(year == used_year) %>% select(region, prob)
  if (nrow(df_year) == 0) return(NULL)

  map_data_df <- mapping_54 %>%
    mutate(
      RegionCode  = as.character(RegionCode),
      CountryCode = as.character(CountryCode),
      CountryCode = ifelse(CountryCode == "SSD", "SDS", CountryCode)
    ) %>%
    left_join(df_year, by = c("RegionCode" = "region"))

  world_data <- world %>% left_join(map_data_df, by = c("iso_key" = "CountryCode"))

  title_suffix <- if (used_year != snap_year)
    sprintf(" (closest year: %d)", used_year) else ""

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = world_data[world_data$iso_key == "ATA", ],
            fill = "grey90", color = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
    geom_sf(data = world_data[world_data$iso_key != "ATA", ],
            aes(fill = prob), color = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors   = c("#e74c3c", "#f39c12", "#f7dc6f", "#2ecc71"),
      limits   = c(0, 1),
      labels   = scales::percent,
      na.value = "grey90",
      name     = "Pr(Adoption)"
    ) +
    geom_sf(
      data = world_data[!is.na(world_data$prob) & world_data$prob >= threshold &
                          world_data$iso_key != "ATA", ],
      fill = NA, color = "#2C3E50", linewidth = 0.4
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text  = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
```
