```{r snapshot-map-helper, include=FALSE}
      title    = sprintf("%s Adoption Probability — %d%s", sector_name, snap_year, title_suffix),
      subtitle = sprintf("Dark outline = regions at or above threshold %.2f | Model: %s",
                         threshold, params$modelName)
    )

  print(p)
  fname <- sprintf("map_prob_%s_%d", tolower(sector_name), snap_year)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, paste0(fname, ".png")), p, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, paste0(fname, ".svg")), p, width = 12, height = 6)
}
```

```{r snapshot-maps-bulk, echo=FALSE, results='asis', fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
cat('<div class="bulk-only">')
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_bulk) && !is.null(mapping_54)) {
  for (yr in snapshot_years) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### Bulk %d\n\n", yr))
    render_snapshot_map(proj_bulk, yr, "Bulk", adoption_threshold)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data or region mapping not available.")
cat('</div>')

cat('<div class="diffuse-only">')
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_diffuse) && !is.null(mapping_54)) {
  for (yr in snapshot_years) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### Diffuse %d\n\n", yr))
    render_snapshot_map(proj_diffuse, yr, "Diffuse", adoption_threshold)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data or region mapping not available.")
cat('</div>')
```

---

# Comparison {.tabset}

This section compares Bulk and Diffuse adoption model results side-by-side. Use it to assess whether the two sectors respond to the same predictors with similar magnitudes.

## Model Fit Comparison

```{r adp-fit-comparison, echo=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=4}
comp_df <- data.frame(
  Sector   = c("Bulk", "Diffuse"),
  PseudoR2 = c(stats_bulk$pseudoR2, stats_diffuse$pseudoR2),
  AIC      = c(stats_bulk$aic, stats_diffuse$aic),
  Quality  = c(
    ifelse(stats_bulk$pseudoR2   >= 0.4, "Good", ifelse(stats_bulk$pseudoR2   >= 0.2, "Moderate", "Poor")),
    ifelse(stats_diffuse$pseudoR2 >= 0.4, "Good", ifelse(stats_diffuse$pseudoR2 >= 0.2, "Moderate", "Poor"))
  )
)
p_r2 <- ggplot(comp_df, aes(x = Sector, y = PseudoR2,
    fill = factor(Quality, levels = c("Poor", "Moderate", "Good")))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", PseudoR2)), vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Poor" = col_bad, "Moderate" = col_warn, "Good" = col_good), name = "Fit quality") +
  labs(title = "McFadden Pseudo-R² by Sector", y = "Pseudo-R²", x = NULL) + ylim(0, 1) + theme_report()
p_aic <- ggplot(comp_df, aes(x = Sector, y = AIC, fill = Sector)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", AIC)), vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bulk" = col_bulk, "Diffuse" = col_diffuse)) +
  labs(title = "AIC by Sector (lower = better)", y = "AIC", x = NULL) +
  theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "none")
library(patchwork)
p_r2 + p_aic + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read:** Pseudo-R² (McFadden) above 0.4 indicates good fit (green); 0.2–0.4 moderate (orange); below 0.2 poor (red). AIC is only comparable within the same sector across different model specifications — lower is better. Both sectors are estimated on different samples (different regions/years have positive prices), so AIC is not directly comparable between Bulk and Diffuse.
</div>

## AME Comparison

```{r adp-ame-comparison, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
make_ame_comparison <- function(fit_b, fit_d) {
  ame_b <- fit_b$ame; ame_d <- fit_d$ame
  if (is.null(ame_b) || is.null(ame_d)) return(invisible(NULL))
  shared <- intersect(ame_b$term, ame_d$term)
  shared <- shared[shared != "(Intercept)"]
  if (length(shared) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))
  df <- data.frame(
    Term             = vapply(shared, clean_term_plain, character(1)),
    `Bulk AME`       = round(ame_b$ame[match(shared, ame_b$term)], 4),
    `Bulk p`         = round(ame_b$p[match(shared, ame_b$term)], 3),
    `Diffuse AME`    = round(ame_d$ame[match(shared, ame_d$term)], 4),
    `Diffuse p`      = round(ame_d$p[match(shared, ame_d$term)], 3),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
  kable(df, format = "html", caption = "AME Comparison — Shared Predictors (probability scale)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
}
make_ame_comparison(fit_bulk, fit_diffuse)
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this table:** Each row is a predictor appearing in both models. Bulk and Diffuse AMEs are on the same probability scale (percentage-point change in Pr(Adoption) per 1-unit increase). Large differences between sectors indicate the predictor drives Bulk and Diffuse adoption differently — this may reflect sector-specific political economy dynamics. Divergent significance (significant in one sector but not the other) also warrants attention.
</div>

---

# Appendix {.tabset}

## Model Formula

```{r model-formula-bulk, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(fit_bulk)) {
  cat('<div class="bulk-only">\n\n**Bulk Adoption Model Equation:**\n\n')
  cat(formatModelEquationLatex(
    coefficients = pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_bulk$coeftest),
    actorPowerIndex = cfg$Bulk$actorIndex,
    actorPowerDrivers = cfg$Bulk$actorPower,
    instQualityDrivers = cfg$Bulk$instQuality,
    controlDrivers = cfg$Bulk$controls,
    depVar = "\\text{logit}\\left(\\text{Pr}(\\text{Bulk Adoption})\\right)"
  ), "\n\n")
  cat("Significance codes: $^{***}\\ p < 0.01$, $^{**}\\ p < 0.05$, $^{*}\\ p < 0.1$.\n")
  cat('</div>\n')
}
```

```{r model-formula-diffuse, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(fit_diffuse)) {
  cat('<div class="diffuse-only">\n\n**Diffuse Adoption Model Equation:**\n\n')
  cat(formatModelEquationLatex(
    coefficients = pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_diffuse$coeftest),
    actorPowerIndex = cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex,
    actorPowerDrivers = cfg$Diffuse$actorPower,
    instQualityDrivers = cfg$Diffuse$instQuality,
    controlDrivers = cfg$Diffuse$controls,
    depVar = "\\text{logit}\\left(\\text{Pr}(\\text{Diffuse Adoption})\\right)"
  ), "\n\n")
  cat("Significance codes: $^{***}\\ p < 0.01$, $^{**}\\ p < 0.05$, $^{*}\\ p < 0.1$.\n")
  cat('</div>\n')
}
```

## Adoption Threshold Guide

```{r calc-alternative-thresholds, echo=FALSE}
calc_thresholds_local <- function(fit_obj) {
  if (is.null(fit_obj) || is.null(fit_obj$model)) return(list(Sens = 0.5, SD = 0.5, FP = 0.5))
  m <- fit_obj$model
  hist_probs <- if (inherits(m, "logistf")) m$predict else m$fitted.values
  actual_outcomes <- m$y
  adopters_prob <- hist_probs[actual_outcomes == 1]
  non_adopters_prob <- hist_probs[actual_outcomes == 0]
  
  t_sens <- if (length(adopters_prob) > 0) quantile(adopters_prob, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE) else 0.5
  t_sd   <- if (length(adopters_prob) > 0) mean(adopters_prob, na.rm = TRUE) - sd(adopters_prob, na.rm = TRUE) else 0.5
  t_fp   <- if (length(non_adopters_prob) > 0) quantile(non_adopters_prob, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) else 0.5
  
  list(
    Sens = round(max(0.01, min(0.99, t_sens)), 3),
    SD   = round(max(0.01, min(0.99, t_sd)), 3),
    FP   = round(max(0.01, min(0.99, t_fp)), 3)
  )
}

thresh_bulk_local <- calc_thresholds_local(fit_bulk)
thresh_diffuse_local <- calc_thresholds_local(fit_diffuse)
```

The default adoption threshold (`r adoption_threshold`) classifies a region as having adopted carbon pricing when the model's predicted probability first exceeds that value.

### Standard Guidance

| Threshold | Interpretation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **0.5** | Standard decision boundary (logit model default) — balanced false positive / false negative rate |
| **< 0.5** | More sensitive — catches early movers but accepts more false positives |
| **> 0.5** | More conservative — higher confidence required before flagging adoption |

### Data-Driven Alternative Thresholds (from Section 10.1 of IAM-PFM Report)

Rather than using an arbitrary threshold like `0.5`, we can calibrate the threshold based on the model's historical predictive performance. Three alternative methods can be used:

1. **Method 1: 90% Historical Sensitivity (Sens)**
   - **Definition:** Finds the threshold where 90% of actual historical adoptions are correctly caught (10th percentile of predicted probabilities among historical adopters).
   - **Rationale:** Minimizes False Negatives (failures to predict adoption when it actually occurred), making it highly sensitive to early signals.
   
2. **Method 2: Standard Deviation Margin (SD)**
   - **Definition:** Mean predicted probability among historical adopters minus one standard deviation.
   - **Rationale:** Includes regions within the normal range of historical early movers, creating a representative threshold for "standard" policy readiness.

3. **Method 3: False Positive Ceiling (FP)**
   - **Definition:** 75th percentile of predicted probabilities among non-adopters.
   - **Rationale:** Accepts some false positives to ensure we do not miss the policy adoption signal, serving as an upper ceiling for classification.

#### Calibrated Threshold Values for Current Model

The calculated values for this specific model configuration (**`r params$modelName`**) are:

| Sector | Method 1: 90% Sensitivity (Sens) | Method 2: Standard Deviation Margin (SD) | Method 3: False Positive Ceiling (FP) |
|--------|----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| **Bulk** | **`r sprintf("%.1f%%", thresh_bulk_local$Sens * 100)`** | **`r sprintf("%.1f%%", thresh_bulk_local$SD * 100)`** | **`r sprintf("%.1f%%", thresh_bulk_local$FP * 100)`** |
| **Diffuse** | **`r sprintf("%.1f%%", thresh_diffuse_local$Sens * 100)`** | **`r sprintf("%.1f%%", thresh_diffuse_local$SD * 100)`** | **`r sprintf("%.1f%%", thresh_diffuse_local$FP * 100)`** |

*Note: You can re-render this report and update projections by setting the `adoptionThreshold` parameter (e.g. `adoptionThreshold = 0.35` or `0.28`) in your render call.*

## Session Information

```{r session, echo=FALSE}
tryCatch(
  print(sessionInfo()),
  error = function(e) {
    cat("R Version:", R.version.string, "\n")
    cat("Platform:", R.version$platform, "\n")
  }
)
```
